**![][image1]**

**Name:** Matt Sha

**Role:** Research Assistant

**Credentials:** Medical Student

**Bio:** Matt Sha is a medical student in the Vancouver Fraser Medical Program at the University of British Columbia. He completed a Bachelor of Science in Integrated Sciences (Human Diseases & Data Analysis) at the University of British Columbia, where he was involved in research projects studying men’s health and mental health. Matt is also a patient partner at BC Cancer and a patient advocate for sarcoma and adolescent and young adult cancer. He enjoys playing basketball, going to the gym, reading, journaling, spending time in nature, and enjoying music.

**Research Interests:** 

* Psychosocial oncology  
* Health promotion  
* Mental health  
* Patient-centred care  
* Qualitative research

**Education:** 

* BSc in Integrated Sciences  
  * University of British Columbia

**![][image2]**  
**Name:** Ralph Justin Uy

**Role:** Research Assistant

**Credentials:** Medical Student

**Bio:** Ralph is a medical student in the Vancouver Fraser Medical Program at the University of British Columbia. He graduated with a Bachelor of Science in Integrated Sciences with a focus on Immunology and Physiology. During his undergraduate studies he worked on research projects in transfusion medicine which sparked his interest in clinical research. Since then, he has become involved in a wider range of projects across different clinical fields. Outside of school, Ralph enjoys powerlifting, playing badminton, and trying new restaurants around Vancouver.

**Research Interests:** 

* AI in Medicine  
* Digital Health  
* Quality Improvement  
* Clinical Outcomes Research  
* Healthcare delivery research

**Education:** 

* BSc in Integrated Sciences  
  * University of British Columbia

**![][image3]**

**Name:** Myles Chan

**Role:** Research Assistant 

**Credentials:** Medical Student

**Bio:** Myles Chan is a medical student in the Vancouver Fraser Medical Program at the University of British Columbia. He holds a Bachelor of Science from the University of Toronto, specializing in Health and Disease with a minor in Immunology. Prior to medical school, he contributed to clinical research at the Centre for Addiction and Mental Health (CAMH), focusing on diverse projects within the scope of substance use disorders. As a dedicated advocate for oncology patients, his two years of volunteer experience at Princess Margaret Hospital informed his current research interests in patient resource navigation. Outside of medicine, Myles is an avid runner and basketball player who also enjoys weightlifting and cooking.

**Research Interests:** 

* AI Use in Medicine  
* Health Equity & Access  
* Clinical Informatics  
* Substance Use Disorders

**Education:** 

* BSc in Human Biology (specializing in Health and Disease, minor in Immunology)  
  * University of Toronto

**![][image4]**  
**Name:** Andrew Loe	

**Role: Research Assistant**

**Credentials:** Medical Student

**Bio:** Andrew Loe is a medical student at the University of British Columbia’s Island Medical Program in Victoria, BC. Originally from Okotoks, Alberta, Andrew has called Victoria home for the past six years. He holds a Bachelor of Science in Biochemistry with a minor in Music from the University of Victoria. Prior to medical school, he worked in a DNA repair lab investigating how RNA modifications modulate genome stability, specifically through the protein Cbf5. Additionally, he worked in an AI cardiology lab focusing on harnessing machine learning for CMR workflows and clinical translation. Outside of his studies, Andrew continues his music education through UVic, enjoys weightlifting, and is an avid chess player.

**Research Interests:** 

* Precision Medicine  
* Clinical AI Integration  
* Translational Oncology  
* Health Equity and Access  
* Digital Health

**Education:** 

*  (Hon.) BSc. In Biochemistry with a minor in music  
  * University of Victoria

![][image5]  
**Name:** Hannah Salcudean

**Role:** Research Assistant

**Credentials:** MS, BSc

**Bio:** Hannah Salcudean is a first-year medical student at the University of British Columbia. Prior to medical school, she completed a Master’s in Neuroimaging and Informatics at the University of Southern California, where she worked on imaging methods to quantify brain iron deposition in hematologic conditions. Hannah previously earned a Bachelor of Science in Combined Major in Science (Computer Science, Life Sciences, and Environmental Science) at UBC. Before starting medical school, Hannah worked as a firmware engineer at Lucid Vision Labs, where she developed an interest in combining computer science and technology with medicine. Outside of school, she enjoys taking her dog, Winston, for hikes and making clay magnets.

**Research Interests:** 

* Computational medicine  
* Medical imaging  
* Digital health  
* AI in healthcare  
* Health equity

**Education:** 

* Master of Science (MS), Neuroimaging and Informatics   
  * University of Southern California  
* Bachelor of Science (BSc), Combined Major in Science (Computer Science, Life Sciences, Environmental Science)
